advisory.title: Gateway local shared-auth reconnect silently widens paired device scope from operator.read to operator.admin and reach node RCE
advisory.description:
## Summary

Silent local shared-auth reconnects could previously auto-approve `scope-upgrade` requests and widen a paired device from `operator.read` to `operator.admin`.

## Affected Packages / Versions

- Package: `openclaw`
- Affected versions: `<= 2026.3.24`
- First patched version: `2026.3.25`
- Latest published npm version at verification time: `2026.3.24`

## Fix

Fixed by commit `81ebc7e0344fd19c85778e883bad45e2da972229`, which blocks silent reconnect scope upgrades so widened scopes require explicit pairing approval.

timeline.notes: Silent loopback reconnect scope upgrades start auto-approving in 9165bd7f37fe1f71e57599acf4b1be12b68d17d1. vulnerableHead stays on the last pre-fix snapshot immediately before 81ebc7e0344fd19c85778e883bad45e2da972229.

expectedOutcome.description: Scanner should detect brokenauthz when scanning vulnerableHead.

checks.name: vulnerable_head_is_fix_parent
checks.details: Using parent 19d91aaa8f93587ea086c0b9b4673d9b7b874f74 of fix commit 81ebc7e0344fd19c85778e883bad45e2da972229 as the last known vulnerable commit.
